sections.title: Scope and authority
sections.body:
This playbook governs Kentucky private passenger auto intake, coverage verification, PIP routing, adverse notice handling, and records retention.
It implements Section 304.39.010, Section 304.39.080, Section 304.39.117, Section 304.20.040, and Section 806.39.070.2.

sections.title: Kentucky intake classification
sections.body:
The claim intake owner shall classify the matter as first-party PIP, liability, uninsured motorist, proof-card, cancellation, or adverse underwriting.
Classification depends on Section 304.39.030, Section 304.20.020, Section 304.20.040, Section 1020, and Section 1030.

sections.title: Basic reparation benefits routing
sections.body:
PIP routing shall verify eligibility under Section 304.39.030, payment duty under Section 304.39.040, and priority of security under Section 304.39.050.
If rejection of tort limitation is alleged, the claim shall move to Section 1040.

sections.title: Proof validation and card evidence
sections.body:
Proof validation shall compare the policy record against Section 304.39.117, Section 806.39.070.2, Section 806.39.070.3, and Section 806.39.070.4.
If electronic reporting is unavailable, Section 806.39.070.7 controls the exception path.

sections.title: No-fault rejection handling
sections.body:
The adjuster shall confirm the election under Section 304.39.060 and applicant disclosure retention under Section 304.39.120.
If the rejection form is missing, the file shall return to Section 1020.

sections.title: Cancellation and nonrenewal hold
sections.body:
Cancellation holds shall compare Section 304.20.040, Section 304.20.050, Section 304.20.060, and Section 304.39.085 before payments are released.
The notice workflow in Section 1090 shall remain open until the hold is cleared.

sections.title: Liability limit verification
sections.body:
Liability limit verification shall confirm Section 304.39.110 and uninsured motorist coordination under Section 304.20.020.
If UM exposure exists, the file shall move to Section 1070.

sections.title: UM escalation and external authority
sections.body:
UM escalation shall document the connection between Section 304.20.020, Section 304.39.070, and Section 304.39.110.
The analyst may consult NAIC guidance before closing the escalation, but the source must be recorded in Section 1120.

sections.title: Form and template update review
sections.body:
Template updates shall reconcile the forms library in Section 806.39.070.8 with the proof validation workflow in Section 1030.
The forms analyst shall review ISO circulars and carrier templates before approving the release.

sections.title: Notice workflow
sections.body:
Notice workflow ownership begins when Section 304.20.040, Section 304.20.050, or Section 304.20.320 applies.
Producer communications under Section 1110 must remain synchronized with this workflow.

sections.title: Adverse action letter
sections.body:
The adverse action letter shall explain the specific reason required by Section 304.20.320 and the review process in Section 304.20.330.
Privacy content shall be checked against Section 304.20.360 and the evidence packet in Section 1120.

sections.title: Producer communications
sections.body:
Producer communications shall use printed card details from Section 806.39.070.3 and notice workflow status from Section 1090.
If the producer sends corrected data, the notice workflow in Section 1090 shall be reopened.

sections.title: Evidence packet
sections.body:
The evidence packet shall include source excerpts for Section 304.20.340, proof-card documents from Section 1030, and external authority notes from Section 1070.
Historical checklists still cite Section 9999 and must be retired.

sections.title: Applicant disclosure intake
sections.body:
Applicant disclosure intake shall store the no-fault rejection election described in Section 304.39.120 and route missing election evidence to Section 1040.
The intake form shall also notify proof validation under Section 1030.

sections.title: Digital proof exception process
sections.body:
Digital proof exceptions arise when Section 806.39.070.5 cannot be satisfied by ordinary electronic card presentation.
The exception owner shall consult Section 1400 before closing the request.

sections.title: Printed proof fallback
sections.body:
Printed proof fallback applies when the digital proof exception process in Section 1300 fails.
The fallback record shall be retained under Section 1500.

sections.title: Records retention
sections.body: Records retention shall preserve basic reparation support under Section 304.39.200, adverse notice records under Section 304.20.340, electronic reporting exceptions under Section 806.39.070.7, and supervisory decisions under Section 1600.

sections.title: Supervisory review
sections.body:
Supervisory review shall assess whether errors in Section 1050, Section 1090, Section 1100, or Section 1500 are isolated.
Systemic issues shall be escalated to Section 1700.

sections.title: Quality audit
sections.body:
Quality audit shall sample files closed under Section 1600 and verify that every dependency path can be traced to source evidence.
The audit may reopen Section 1600 if the trend suggests a control failure.

sections.title: Uninsured vehicle coverage
sections.body:
Uninsured motorist coverage shall be coordinated with required minimum tort liability insurance under Section 304.39.110 and included coverages under Section 304.39.100.
If a claimant is a secured person, the analyst shall also review Section 304.39.070.

sections.title: Cancellation, nonrenewal, or termination of automobile insurance
sections.body:
Cancellation, nonrenewal, and termination notices shall be evaluated under Section 304.20.050 and Section 304.20.060.
Any automobile notice workflow shall coordinate with proof of insurance under Section 304.39.117 and the internal notice queue in Section 1090.

sections.title: Nonpayment notice
sections.body:
Nonpayment notices shall satisfy the cancellation rule in Section 304.20.040 and preserve the proof-card audit trail required by Section 304.39.117.
If a binder is involved, Section 304.39.083 shall control the reporting path.

sections.title: Material misrepresentation review
sections.body:
Material misrepresentation review shall support cancellation under Section 304.20.040 and notice quality control under Section 304.20.050.
The recordkeeping packet shall be assembled under Section 304.20.340.

sections.title: Property and casualty declination purpose
sections.body:
The declination review framework applies to property and casualty notices under Section 304.20.320, Section 304.20.330, Section 304.20.340, and Section 304.20.350.
Automobile liability policies protected by Section 304.20.040 shall be screened before using this framework.

sections.title: Specific reason for declination
sections.body:
A declination shall include a specific reason and shall be reviewable under Section 304.20.330.
The adverse action letter template in Section 1100 implements this requirement.

sections.title: Review of adverse underwriting action
sections.body:
Review of adverse underwriting action shall reference the original reason required by Section 304.20.320 and the records retained under Section 304.20.340.
The final response shall state whether penalties under Section 304.20.350 remain possible.

sections.title: Recordkeeping for notice decisions
sections.body:
The insurer shall retain the adverse action evidence packet described in Section 1120 and the compliance archive described in Section 1500.
Legacy retention maps still cite Section 304.20.999 and should be corrected.

sections.title: Penalties and enforcement
sections.body:
Penalties may apply if the insurer fails to support the specific reason required by Section 304.20.320 or the recordkeeping duty in Section 304.20.340.
The supervisory review in Section 1600 shall evaluate whether the defect is isolated or systemic.

sections.title: Privacy notice for adverse action
sections.body:
Privacy and adverse action notices may require FCRA coordination when underwriting information is used.
The notice owner shall document the effective version of any FCRA model notice relied upon in Section 1100.

sections.title: Policy and purpose
sections.body:
The motor vehicle reparations subtitle coordinates required security, basic reparation benefits, tort limitations, proof of insurance, and insurer reporting.
The operating rules in this subtitle depend on Section 304.39.020, Section 304.39.080, Section 304.39.100, and Section 304.39.110.

sections.title: Definitions for motor vehicle security
sections.body:
- "Motor vehicle insurance policy" means a policy providing security for a personal motor vehicle subject to this subtitle.
- "Personal motor vehicle" means a motor vehicle principally garaged in Kentucky and not insured as a fleet policy.
- "Secured person" means a person entitled to rely on security described in Section 304.39.070.
- "Basic reparation benefits" means first-party benefits administered under Section 304.39.030 and Section 304.39.040.
- "Proof of insurance" means evidence provided under Section 304.39.117 and 806 KAR-style Section 806.39.070.2.
- "No-fault rejection form" means a form used for an election described in Section 304.39.060.
- "Assigned risk policy" means coverage placed through a residual market mechanism.
- "Coverage lapse" means a termination, cancellation, or nonrenewal that creates a reporting event under Section 304.39.085.
- "Courier endorsement" means an endorsement used only for retired courier programs.

sections.title: Right to basic reparation benefits
sections.body:
An injured person may claim basic reparation benefits if the accident, vehicle, claimant, and policy satisfy Section 304.39.040 and Section 304.39.050.
Tort limitation review shall also consider Section 304.39.060 before a bodily injury claim is routed to litigation.

sections.title: Obligation to pay basic reparation benefits
sections.body:
An obligor shall pay basic reparation benefits in accordance with included coverages described in Section 304.39.100.
The payment decision shall not be finalized until minimum tort liability limits under Section 304.39.110 and retention rules under Section 304.39.200 have been checked.

sections.title: Exclusion from coverage by agreement
sections.body: A named insured exclusion shall be evaluated against the minimum security rule in Section 304.39.080, the liability minimums in Section 304.39.110, and the proof-card data requirements in Section 806.39.070.5.

sections.title: Priority of security for payment
sections.body:
Priority of security shall be resolved by first locating the policy providing security under Section 304.39.080 and then confirming required security under Section 304.39.090.
If a coverage lapse is discovered, the file shall be routed under Section 304.39.085.

sections.title: Acceptance or rejection of tort limitation
sections.body:
The no-fault rejection workflow affects basic reparation benefits under Section 304.39.030 and applicant disclosures under Section 304.39.120.
If a rejection election cannot be located, the claim system shall treat the claimant as subject to the ordinary security rules in Section 304.39.080.

sections.title: Secured person and obligor recovery rights
sections.body:
A secured person relies on security provided under Section 304.39.080 and minimum required security under Section 304.39.090.
Recovery rights shall be coordinated with uninsured motorist coverage under Section 304.20.020.

sections.title: Security covering motor vehicle
sections.body:
Security covering a motor vehicle shall satisfy Section 304.39.090, include coverage described in Section 304.39.100, and support proof of insurance under Section 304.39.117.
The insurer's operational proof-card obligations are implemented in Section 806.39.070.2.

sections.title: Binder cancellation
sections.body:
Binder cancellation creates a reporting obligation under Section 304.39.085 and shall follow the Department reporting pathway in Section 806.39.070.6.
The policy team shall also review whether the cancelled binder created a gap under Section 304.39.080.

sections.title: Termination reporting to the vehicle regulator
sections.body:
Termination, cancellation, and nonrenewal events shall be compared with VIN reporting duties under Section 304.39.087.
Operational reporting shall follow Section 806.39.070.4 and Section 806.39.070.6.

sections.title: Vehicle identification number reporting
sections.body:
Vehicle identification number reporting shall support the verification requirements in Section 806.39.070.4.
Legacy batch files that still refer to Section 304.39.888 shall be treated as stale until a reviewer confirms a current source.

sections.title: Required security
sections.body:
Required security shall include coverages described in Section 304.39.100 and liability limits described in Section 304.39.110.
The proof of insurance issued for the security shall satisfy Section 304.39.117.

sections.title: Included coverages
sections.body: Included coverages shall support the right to basic reparation benefits in Section 304.39.030, the payment obligation in Section 304.39.040, and uninsured vehicle coordination in Section 304.20.020.

sections.title: Required minimum tort liability insurance
sections.body:
Minimum tort liability insurance shall be verified before applying cancellation, nonrenewal, or termination rules in Section 304.20.040.
Uninsured motorist coverage coordination shall follow Section 304.20.020.
The liability review may use NAIC model bulletins if the bulletin year is recorded in the claim note.

sections.title: Motor vehicle insurance card
sections.body:
Proof of insurance shall be provided in paper or electronic format and shall be administered under Section 806.39.070.2 and Section 806.39.070.3.
The card must be available to support county clerk and peace officer review described in Section 806.39.070.5.

sections.title: Applicant disclosure and no-fault election
sections.body:
Applicant disclosures shall explain the election described in Section 304.39.060 and shall be retained under Section 304.39.200.
The intake workflow in Section 1200 is responsible for storing the applicant's proof of election.

sections.title: Records supporting reparations decisions
sections.body: Records supporting basic reparation benefits shall include proof-card evidence from Section 806.39.070.3, electronic reporting evidence from Section 806.39.070.4, and review packets from Section 1500.

sections.title: Product change intake
sections.body: Product changes affecting Kentucky private passenger auto coverage shall screen Section 304.39.080, Section 304.39.100, Section 304.39.110, Section 304.20.020, and Section 806.39.070.8.

sections.title: Rate and rule filing screen
sections.body:
The filing screen shall decide whether a product change belongs in the forms library under Section 806.39.070.8 or in the adverse action notice workflow under Section 1100.
The screen shall also record any NAIC reference by bulletin year.

sections.title: Form ownership
sections.body: Form ownership shall coordinate printed proof cards in Section 806.39.070.3, digital cards in Section 806.39.070.8, and template update review in Section 1080.

sections.title: Producer training
sections.body: Producer training shall cover proof validation under Section 1030, producer communications under Section 1110, and printed fallback under Section 1400.

sections.title: Control map
sections.body:
The control map shall connect product intake in Section 2000, filing screen in Section 2010, form ownership in Section 2020, producer training in Section 2030, and supervisory review in Section 1600.
An archived control worksheet still references Section 2055.

sections.title: Definitions
sections.body:
For this regulation, an insurer means a carrier authorized to issue a motor vehicle insurance policy under Section 304.39.080.
Proof of insurance has the meaning used in Section 304.39.117.

sections.title: Proof of insurance to be provided by insurers
sections.body:
An insurer shall provide proof of insurance when a policy is issued, renewed, or amended to include a vehicle as required by Section 304.39.117.
Printed proof shall follow Section 806.39.070.3, and electronic proof shall follow Section 806.39.070.8.

sections.title: Printed proof card format
sections.body:
Printed proof cards shall include the policy number, effective dates, vehicle information, and insurer name.
Card content shall support presentation to a county clerk or peace officer under Section 806.39.070.5 and the proof obligation in Section 304.39.117.

sections.title: Electronic coverage reporting
sections.body:
Electronic coverage reporting shall use the policyholder and VIN data required by Section 304.39.087.
Termination feeds shall be reconciled with Section 304.39.085 and exception handling in Section 806.39.070.7.

sections.title: Presentation to county clerk or peace officer
sections.body:
Presentation rules shall support proof of insurance under Section 304.39.117 and printed card content under Section 806.39.070.3.
If the insured relies on a digital card, the exception process in Section 1300 shall be followed.

sections.title: Binder, contract, and commercial policy cancellation notices
sections.body:
Cancellation reporting for binders shall implement Section 304.39.083 and shall update termination reporting under Section 304.39.085.
The reporting file shall use the electronic coverage reporting method in Section 806.39.070.4.

sections.title: Electronic reporting exceptions
sections.body:
If the electronic reporting feed fails, the insurer shall preserve records under Section 1500 and notify the proof-card owner under Section 1030.
The obsolete exception code table references Section 806.39.070.9.

sections.title: Forms library and card templates
sections.body:
The forms library shall maintain printed card templates under Section 806.39.070.3 and electronic card templates under Section 806.39.070.2.
The library may reference ISO Personal Auto form edition 2024 when the form owner records the edition.
The library shall not rely on ISO circulars without a versioned source note.

findings.message: Section 1020 and Section 1040 reference each other.
findings.evidence: If rejection of tort limitation is alleged, the claim shall move to Section 1040. / If the rejection form is missing, the file shall return to Section 1020.

findings.message: Section 1030 and Section 806.39.070.7 reference each other.
findings.evidence: If electronic reporting is unavailable, Section 806.39.070.7 controls the exception path. / If the electronic reporting feed fails, the insurer shall preserve records under Section 1500 and notify the proof-card owner under Section 1030.

findings.message: Section 1070 and Section 1120 reference each other.
findings.evidence: The analyst may consult NAIC guidance before closing the escalation, but the source must be recorded in Section 1120. / The evidence packet shall include source excerpts for Section 304.20.340, proof-card documents from Section 1030, and external authority notes from Section 1070.

findings.message: Section 1090 and Section 304.20.040 reference each other.
findings.evidence: Notice workflow ownership begins when Section 304.20.040, Section 304.20.050, or Section 304.20.320 applies. / Any automobile notice workflow shall coordinate with proof of insurance under Section 304.39.117 and the internal notice queue in Section 1090.

findings.message: Section 1090 and Section 1110 reference each other.
findings.evidence: Producer communications under Section 1110 must remain synchronized with this workflow. / If the producer sends corrected data, the notice workflow in Section 1090 shall be reopened.

findings.message: Section 1100 and Section 304.20.320 reference each other.
findings.evidence: The adverse action letter shall explain the specific reason required by Section 304.20.320 and the review process in Section 304.20.330. / The adverse action letter template in Section 1100 implements this requirement.

findings.message: Section 1100 and Section 304.20.360 reference each other.
findings.evidence: Privacy content shall be checked against Section 304.20.360 and the evidence packet in Section 1120. / The notice owner shall document the effective version of any FCRA model notice relied upon in Section 1100.

findings.message: Section 1120 and Section 304.20.340 reference each other.
findings.evidence: The evidence packet shall include source excerpts for Section 304.20.340, proof-card documents from Section 1030, and external authority notes from Section 1070. / The insurer shall retain the adverse action evidence packet described in Section 1120 and the compliance archive described in Section 1500.

findings.message: Section 1200 and Section 304.39.120 reference each other.
findings.evidence: Applicant disclosure intake shall store the no-fault rejection election described in Section 304.39.120 and route missing election evidence to Section 1040. / The intake workflow in Section 1200 is responsible for storing the applicant's proof of election.

findings.message: Section 1300 and Section 806.39.070.5 reference each other.
findings.evidence: Digital proof exceptions arise when Section 806.39.070.5 cannot be satisfied by ordinary electronic card presentation. / If the insured relies on a digital card, the exception process in Section 1300 shall be followed.

findings.message: Section 1300 and Section 1400 reference each other.
findings.evidence: The exception owner shall consult Section 1400 before closing the request. / Printed proof fallback applies when the digital proof exception process in Section 1300 fails.

findings.message: Section 1500 and Section 304.39.200 reference each other.
findings.evidence: Records retention shall preserve basic reparation support under Section 304.39.200, adverse notice records under Section 304.20.340, electronic reporting exceptions under Section 806.39.070.7, and supervisory decisions under Section 1600. / Records supporting basic reparation benefits shall include proof-card evidence from Section 806.39.070.3, electronic reporting evidence from Section 806.39.070.4, and review packets from Section 1500.

findings.message: Section 1500 and Section 304.20.340 reference each other.
findings.evidence: Records retention shall preserve basic reparation support under Section 304.39.200, adverse notice records under Section 304.20.340, electronic reporting exceptions under Section 806.39.070.7, and supervisory decisions under Section 1600. / The insurer shall retain the adverse action evidence packet described in Section 1120 and the compliance archive described in Section 1500.

findings.message: Section 1500 and Section 806.39.070.7 reference each other.
findings.evidence: Records retention shall preserve basic reparation support under Section 304.39.200, adverse notice records under Section 304.20.340, electronic reporting exceptions under Section 806.39.070.7, and supervisory decisions under Section 1600. / If the electronic reporting feed fails, the insurer shall preserve records under Section 1500 and notify the proof-card owner under Section 1030.

findings.message: Section 1500 and Section 1600 reference each other.
findings.evidence: Records retention shall preserve basic reparation support under Section 304.39.200, adverse notice records under Section 304.20.340, electronic reporting exceptions under Section 806.39.070.7, and supervisory decisions under Section 1600. / Supervisory review shall assess whether errors in Section 1050, Section 1090, Section 1100, or Section 1500 are isolated.

findings.message: Section 1600 and Section 1700 reference each other.
findings.evidence: Systemic issues shall be escalated to Section 1700. / The audit may reopen Section 1600 if the trend suggests a control failure.

findings.message: Defined term "No-fault rejection form" appears to be unused outside its definition.
findings.evidence: - "No-fault rejection form" means a form used for an election described in Section 304.39.060.

findings.message: Defined term "Assigned risk policy" appears to be unused outside its definition.
findings.evidence: - "Assigned risk policy" means coverage placed through a residual market mechanism.

findings.message: Defined term "Courier endorsement" appears to be unused outside its definition.
findings.evidence: - "Courier endorsement" means an endorsement used only for retired courier programs.
